name: Tasker MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for Android Tasker — the powerful Android automation app. Enables AI agents to trigger Tasker tasks and profiles on Android devices remotely, sending commands that execute device automation sequences like controlling settings, launching apps, sending notifications, and running custom workflows on Android.
use_cases: Triggering Tasker automation tasks on Android from AI agent workflows
Remotely controlling Android device settings and apps via Tasker integration
Building AI-driven home automation that includes Android device control via Tasker
Automating Android workflows in response to external events detected by AI agents
not_for: Users without Tasker installed on their Android device
Enterprise MDM device management (use dedicated MDM solutions)
best_when: A power user or developer using Tasker for Android automation wants AI agents to trigger Tasker tasks remotely — connecting AI workflows to physical Android device actions.
avoid_when: You don't have Tasker on an Android device, or you need iOS automation. Also: Tasker requires Tasker HTTP request task setup for remote triggers.

auth.notes: Tasker HTTP task must be configured on the Android device. API key or token for the HTTP endpoint if configured.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — Tasker app is a one-time purchase ($3.49); MCP server is free
pricing.notes: Tasker app is a one-time purchase from Google Play. MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: Depends on Android device performance and network connectivity

known_agent_gotchas: PHYSICAL EFFECTS: Triggers real Android device actions — test Tasker tasks thoroughly before autonomous agent use
Requires Tasker HTTP task configured on Android device for remote triggering — non-trivial setup
Android device must be on same network or accessible via internet — connectivity dependency
Tasker task names must match exactly — no fuzzy matching or discovery
agent_readiness.security_notes: PHYSICAL EFFECTS: Controls real Android device. Secure the Tasker HTTP endpoint with authentication. Device access from network — ensure proper firewall/VPN protection.

security_score_components.security_notes: Physical device control. HTTP endpoint must be secured. Use VPN or authenticated tunnel for remote access.
